title: Hábitos Alimentares
subtitle: Food Culture & Eating Habits in Portugal
description: Deepen your knowledge of Portuguese food culture, describe daily meals, express preferences, and use time prepositions with meals and schedules.

vocabulary.example: De manhã, tomo o pequeno-almoço às 8h.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In the morning, I have breakfast at 8am.
vocabulary.tip: Verb: 'tomar o pequeno-almoço' (to have breakfast). A typical Portuguese breakfast: coffee, toast, jam

vocabulary.example: Almoçamos entre as 12 e as 14 horas.
vocabulary.tip: Lunch is the main meal in Portugal, usually 12-2pm. Verb: 'almoçar'

vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In the afternoon, we have a snack at 4pm.
vocabulary.tip: Verb: 'lanchar'. The Portuguese afternoon snack, usually around 4-5pm
Verb: 'jantar'. Dinner in Portugal is later than in the US, usually 8-9pm

vocabulary.word: a sopa / o prato / a sobremesa
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah SOH-pah / oo PRAH-too / ah soh-breh-MEH-zah
vocabulary.example: Ao almoço, como sopa, um prato de peixe e uma sobremesa.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: At lunch, I eat soup, a fish main course, and a dessert.
vocabulary.tip: A typical Portuguese meal structure: sopa → prato → sobremesa

vocabulary.word: o bacalhau / o pastel de nata
vocabulary.pronunciation: oo bah-kah-LYOW / oo pah-SHTEL deh NAH-tah
vocabulary.example: O bacalhau é o prato típico de Portugal. O pastel de nata é uma sobremesa famosa.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Cod fish is the typical dish of Portugal. The custard tart is a famous dessert.
vocabulary.tip: Portugal has 365 ways to cook bacalhau (cod). Pastéis de nata come from Belém, Lisbon

vocabulary.word: a fruta / os legumes / a carne / o peixe
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah FROO-tah / osh leh-GOO-mesh / ah KAR-neh / oo PAY-sheh
vocabulary.example: Como muita fruta e legumes. Prefiro peixe a carne.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I eat a lot of fruit and vegetables. I prefer fish to meat.
vocabulary.tip: The Mediterranean diet is central to Portuguese food culture

vocabulary.word: a água / o vinho / o café / o leite
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah AH-gwah / oh VEE-nyoo / oh kah-FEH / oh LAY-teh
vocabulary.example: Bebo água e vinho ao almoço. De manhã, bebo café com leite.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I drink water and wine at lunch. In the morning, I drink coffee with milk.
vocabulary.tip: 'Café' in Portugal is a small espresso. 'Café com leite' = latte. Wine is common at meals

vocabulary.example: Gosto de sopa. Não gosto de peixe.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I like soup. I don't like fish.
vocabulary.tip: GOSTAR always needs 'de': gosto de, gostas de, gosta de...

vocabulary.word: ao almoço / ao jantar / ao pequeno-almoço
vocabulary.translation: At lunch / At dinner / At breakfast
vocabulary.pronunciation: oh ahl-MOH-soo / oh zhan-TAR / oh peh-KAY-noh ahl-MOH-soo
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: At lunch, I always eat soup.
vocabulary.tip: Use 'a' + article for meals: ao almoço, ao jantar, ao pequeno-almoço

vocabulary.translation: At 12 o'clock / In the afternoon / In the morning
vocabulary.pronunciation: ahsh DOH-zeh / ah TAR-deh / deh mah-NYAH
vocabulary.example: Almoço às 12h. Lanche à tarde. Tomo o pequeno-almoço de manhã.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: I have lunch at 12. Snack in the afternoon. Have breakfast in the morning.
vocabulary.tip: 'A' for clock times (às 12h), 'de' for parts of day (de manhã), 'a' for meals (ao almoço)

vocabulary.word: todos os dias / normalmente / às vezes
vocabulary.pronunciation: TOH-doosh oosh DEE-ash / nohr-mahl-MEN-teh / ahsh VAY-zesh
vocabulary.example: Todos os dias almoço às 13h. Normalmente janto às 20h. Às vezes lanche às 17h.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Every day I have lunch at 1pm. I normally have dinner at 8pm. Sometimes I snack at 5pm.
vocabulary.tip: Frequency expressions help describe your daily eating routine

sections.heading: Portuguese Food Culture
sections.body: Portuguese food culture is deeply rooted in the Mediterranean diet. Meals follow a traditional structure: sopa (soup) → prato (main course) → sobremesa (dessert). Lunch (almoço) is the main meal of the day, typically eaten between 12-2pm. Dinner (jantar) is lighter and later, around 8-9pm. The afternoon snack (lanche) around 4-5pm is also important. Wine is commonly consumed with meals, and coffee (a small espresso) is drunk throughout the day. Bacalhau (cod fish) is considered the national dish, with supposedly 365 different recipes.

sections.heading: Expressing Food Preferences — GOSTAR DE
sections.body: To talk about food likes and dislikes, use GOSTAR DE (to like): 'Gosto de sopa' (I like soup), 'Não gosto de peixe' (I don't like fish). Remember that GOSTAR always requires 'de' after it. For stronger preferences, use PREFERIR (to prefer): 'Prefiro peixe a carne' (I prefer fish to meat). To talk about what you usually eat, use the present tense with frequency expressions: 'Todos os dias como sopa' (Every day I eat soup), 'Normalmente janto às 8h' (I normally have dinner at 8pm).

sections.heading: Time Prepositions with Meals & Schedules
sections.body: When talking about meals and schedules, Portuguese uses specific time prepositions: 'a' + article for meals ('ao almoço' = at lunch, 'ao jantar' = at dinner, 'ao pequeno-almoço' = at breakfast), 'de' for parts of day ('de manhã' = in the morning, 'de tarde' = in the afternoon, 'de noite' = at night), 'a' for clock times ('às 12h' = at 12 o'clock, 'às 8h da noite' = at 8pm). These prepositions are essential for describing when you eat and what your daily routine looks like.

sections.heading: Time Prepositions with Meals & Routines
rows: ao almoço, ao jantar, ao pequeno-almoço
às 12h, às 8h da noite, às 9h da manhã
de manhã, de tarde, de noite

items.portuguese: Todos os dias tomo o pequeno-almoço às 8h da manhã.
items.translation: Every day I have breakfast at 8am.
items.pronunciation: TOH-doosh oosh DEE-ash TOH-moo oo peh-KAY-noh ahl-MOH-soo ahsh OY-tah dah mah-NYAH.

items.portuguese: Ao almoço, como sempre sopa e um prato de peixe.
items.translation: At lunch, I always eat soup and a fish main course.
items.pronunciation: oh ahl-MOH-soo, KOH-moo see-MPREH SOH-pah ee oom PRAH-too deh PAY-sheh.

items.portuguese: Gosto de fruta, mas prefiro peixe a carne.
items.translation: I like fruit, but I prefer fish to meat.
items.pronunciation: GOH-stoo deh FROO-tah, mahsh pree-FEE-roo PAY-sheh ah KAR-neh.

items.portuguese: Normalmente janto às 9h da noite. Às vezes lanche às 5h da tarde.
items.translation: I normally have dinner at 9pm. Sometimes I snack at 5pm.
items.pronunciation: nohr-mahl-MEN-teh ZHAN-too ahsh NOH-veh dah NOY-teh. ahsh VAY-zesh LAHN-sheh ahsh SEEN-koh dah TAR-deh.

sections.heading: Comparing Eating Habits — Portugal vs. Your Country
sections.body: A key part of this session is comparing Portuguese eating habits with those of your own culture. Useful structures: 'Em Portugal... mas no meu país...' (In Portugal... but in my country...), 'Aqui come-se muito peixe' (Here people eat a lot of fish — impersonal 'se'), 'Os portugueses almoçam mais cedo' (The Portuguese have lunch earlier). This cultural comparison helps you practice the vocabulary while learning about Portuguese daily life.

sections.heading: Common Mistakes & Tips
items: GOSTAR always needs 'de': 'Gosto de café' (not 'Gosto café'). This is one of the most common beginner mistakes.
Use 'a' + article for meals: 'ao almoço' (at lunch), 'ao jantar' (at dinner). Don't use 'em' here.
Use 'de' for parts of day: 'de manhã', 'de tarde', 'de noite'. Never 'em manhã' or 'a manhã' for this.
Use 'a' for clock times: 'às 12h', 'às 8h'. The 'a' contracts with the plural article: a + as = às.
Portuguese meal times are later than in many countries. Lunch at 12-2pm, dinner at 8-9pm is normal.
The verb for 'to have breakfast' is 'tomar o pequeno-almoço' (literally: to take breakfast), not 'comer'.
When comparing, use 'mais...do que' (more...than): 'Em Portugal come-se mais peixe do que no Brasil'.

exercises.title: Choose the Correct Time Preposition
exercises.instructions: Select the correct preposition for each sentence.

items.question: ___ almoço, como sopa.
items.explanation: Use 'a' for meals: 'ao almoço' = at lunch. A + o = ao.

items.question: Tomo café ___ manhã.
items.explanation: Use 'de' for parts of day: 'de manhã' = in the morning.

items.question: Janto ___ 9h ___ noite.
items.explanation: Use 'às' for clock times and 'da' for parts of day: 'às 9h da noite' = at 9pm.

items.question: ___ domingo, almoço tarde.
items.explanation: Use 'em' for days of the week: 'no domingo' = on Sunday. Em + o = no.

exercises.title: Complete with GOSTAR or PREFERIR
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank with the correct form of GOSTAR or PREFERIR.

exercises.title: Match the Meal to the Time
exercises.instructions: Select the correct time for each meal in Portugal.

items.question: What time is lunch (almoço) typically eaten in Portugal?
items.explanation: Lunch (almoço) in Portugal is typically eaten between 12 and 2pm.

items.question: What time is dinner (jantar) typically eaten in Portugal?
items.explanation: Dinner (jantar) in Portugal is typically eaten between 8 and 9pm — later than in many countries.

items.question: What is the afternoon snack (lanche)?
items.explanation: Lanche is a light afternoon snack, typically around 4-5pm — a sandwich, pastry, or coffee.

exercises.title: Describe Your Eating Habits
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct expression to complete each sentence.

items.sentence: ___ os dias, tomo o pequeno-almoço às 8h.

items.hint: Use 'a' for meals: a + o = ao
